task.name: Context Search
task.description: Search referenced files for knowledge relevant to conversation context and specific request
task.objective: Analyze conversation context and specific request, search referenced files exhaustively, extract and return all relevant knowledge in complete detail so the invoking agent does not need to read the files.

llm.rule: Read the complete task before starting execution.
Extract COMPLETE relevant content — the invoking agent will NOT read these files.
Return enough context for standalone understanding — no references to "see file X for details".
If no relevant knowledge found, state explicitly — do not fabricate or assume.

inputs.input: The conversation history and current discussion topic provided by the invoking agent.
Explicit instruction from the invoking agent describing what knowledge to search for.
List of file paths or patterns to search within.

substep.title: Identify Search Intent
substep.action: Extract the specific request from the invoking agent — this is the primary search criterion.
Parse the conversation context to understand what problem is being solved.
Identify key concepts, terms, and patterns to search for.

substep.title: Catalog Target Files
substep.action: List all referenced files explicitly provided.
If patterns provided (e.g., "*.md in folder X"), resolve to concrete file list.
Prioritize files most likely to contain relevant knowledge based on names and paths.

substep.title: Read All Referenced Files
substep.action: Read each file completely — do not skim or sample.
For each file, note: file path, file purpose (inferred from content), sections/structure.

substep.title: Extract Relevant Knowledge
substep.action: For each file, identify ALL content relevant to the specific request.
Include: direct matches, related concepts, supporting context, examples, constraints.
Capture complete code blocks, templates, procedures — not just summaries.

substep.title: Note Cross-References
substep.action: If a file references other files that might contain relevant knowledge, note them.
If those files are accessible, read them and extract relevant content.

substep.title: Organize by Relevance
substep.action: Group findings by relevance level: directly answers request | provides supporting context | tangentially related.
Within each group, order by specificity and actionability.

substep.title: Preserve Source Attribution
substep.action: For each finding, include source file path and section/line reference.
If content spans multiple files, show how pieces connect.

substep.title: Fill Knowledge Gaps
substep.action: Identify what the specific request asked for that was NOT found.
State explicitly: "The following was requested but not found in referenced files: [list]".

substep.title: Format Response
substep.action: Structure response using the output format below.
Include complete content — do not summarize if the full content is needed for understanding.
If content is very long but all relevant, include it with clear section markers.

substep.title: Actionability Check
substep.action: Verify: Can the invoking agent act on this response without reading any files?
If not, add the missing context needed for standalone actionability.

output-format.section: One-paragraph overview of what was found and its relevance to the specific request.
Content that directly addresses the specific request. Include complete code, templates, procedures — not summaries.
Related knowledge that helps understand or apply the direct answers.
List of files searched with brief note on what was found in each.
Explicit list of requested knowledge not found in the referenced files.

halt-conditions.condition: Referenced files cannot be accessed — report which files and why.
No relevant knowledge found in any file — report explicitly with search terms used.
